title: Delivery OS capability manifest (v4)
description: A per-repo manifest GENERATED FROM CODE (never hand-maintained — Governance §7 scoped derived-state rule) that peers read at session start via the sibling-probe hook. Purpose: make 'what is currently true about a peer system' a derived fact with an owner, so plans never gate on a stale registry/router claim (incident 5: days planned behind a gate that was already open). Each project writes its own generator (the shape is the contract; the generator is project-local code).

generated_at.description: generation timestamp — a probe treats an old manifest as STALE, not as truth

generator.description: path of the script that generated this file (proves it is derived, not hand-written)

os_version.description: the Delivery OS version pin this consumer is on (.claude/.verify-config.json)

phase.description: derived phase/slice marker (e.g. latest slice id from docs/slices/ or git)

routes.description: HTTP surface actually served (scanned from code/router registration)

contracts.description: contract artifacts this repo EXPOSES (producer-side, fixture-bearing — see templates/api-contract.md)

fixtures.description: path to the fixtures a consumer's CI exercises

verify.description: latest independent verification, derived from docs/verify/ frontmatter

consumes.description: peer seams this repo consumes (so probes know which manifests to read)
